title: Hydrogen Hopes and Tariff Troubles: Plug Power's Path Forward
text: Plug Power's operational improvements face headwinds from escalating U.S. trade tariffs that threaten to increase component costs. Companies across the hydrogen value chain face shrinking margins as they absorb tariff-related cost increases while trying to remain competitive in global markets. Domestic manufacturers still rely on global supply chains, making them vulnerable to tariff impacts. The global supply chain for PEM components remains a bottleneck, though Plug has made strides in vertical integration to mitigate these risks. US green hydrogen developers face decreased product range and accessibility across the suite of electrolyzer technologies, which poses a challenge to finding the most compatible technology for different projects. Bloom Energy dominates the stationary fuel cell market with 40% gross margin, while Plug's margins remain thinner. Tariffs deter foreign investments in the US hydrogen market, particularly from Asia-Pacific players who dominate electrolyzer and fuel cell production. For investors, PLUG remains a high-risk, high-reward binary play dependent on navigating legal challenges and capitalizing on AI sector energy demands.
